item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*Sheds tear of joy while saluting particle trail of SCP-3836-28*
Godspeed, 3836. Godspeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this so much. I want fast gorilla to be my best friend. fast enough, indeed. that'll do, gorilla. that'll do. 😭

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: There is no indication that image which was used in this article was released under Creative Commons. As such, it is to be assumed it is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He used a hidden entrance he had built, they didn't realize what he was doing until it was too late to stop him.

item.title: Re: Personal Head Cannon
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this. I wish you can +1 comments.

item.title: Re: Personal Head Cannon
item.content:encoded: This is now canon.

item.title: Personal Head Cannon
item.content:encoded:
I like to think that SCP-3836 was once Albert II, the second monkey to go into space. While riding his rocket to space, going faster than any other monkey had gone, he felt the direct sunlight on his body he finally understood what true speed was. In that moment he vowed to one day outrun even the sun.

Official USSR reports say Albert II died on impact after a parachute malfunction. In truth, there was no malfunction. Albert II disabled the parachute himself, as parachutes only slow things down, and he needed to go fast.

item.title: Somewhere, there's a Sonic the Hedgehog reference...
item.content:encoded:
Okay, it's basically everywhere, lolz

Gotta go fast, fast enough, indeed.

This was a fantastic read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was really great read! It was funny to, but I still don't understand exactly how 3836 would enter the rocket, wouldn't he be watched over by somebody? Or did I miss something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have succeeded in your job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just absolutely fantastic and atrociously underrated. Very, very strong +1 for that crazy gorilla.

(I will echo Nicolini's comment above to say that if you're browsing New Posts and see this, please treat yourself and give this one a read. It's criminal how low its rating is.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha you served what you promised :D great!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this SCP, It's nice to see a more lighthearted one once in a while. I am a fan of the anomalous properties being explored through testing logs and the ending is also really sweet, a solid +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I'm ashamed it took me until today to experience this glory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Danke, fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting one. Cute monkey :)

Just found a bug. The year in Addendum 1 is 2017, but in the revision of the containment procedure, it is 2016.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. I have no idea how it isn’t rated higher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes I come back to this article just for a laugh.

If you're scrolling through this in New Posts, please read this article. It's like Joey Fucknuts turned to 11.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really is. Bonus points for feels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I really probably should put him as neutralized, due to him blasting off into deep space, but that would be sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was filled with too many clever ideas that sparked a chuckle to not upvote.

My question: “If this oil is not changed at least once every 6 weeks, SCP-3836 will enter a comatose state until new oil is provided.” Wouldn’t that mean the beloved grease monkey could be considered Neutralized, as neither he nor the D-class will be able to indefinitely provide new oil in his current state?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I LOVED the fast speech, LOVED the interview, LOVE this SCP. +FAST

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because even though Keter represents containment difficulty and not actual danger, people still often read it as such. The Grease Monkey is not dangerous, he is a friend. Plus, I personally think it might have spoiled the ending a little too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made another one that falls into that category, SCP-3516. Check it out, I hope you enjoy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, my bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't really sold on this to begin with. Then, this happened.

Dr Sambre: You seem to have… severe injuries on the top half of your body. where did you get them, and how have you survived?

SCP-3836: Oh, I lean out window when go big fast. No know how fast, dial go boom after say lots of nines.

Dr. Sambre: …And you survived this?

SCP-3836: Is okay, I wear goggles.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love lighthearted test log SCPs. This is up there with 914.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I caught a mistake: you say the model of Toyota it used was 2018, but it escaped containment in 2016. 2017 is the latest model of car it could have used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why does it continue to be Euclid class, when it appears to have actually escaped containment completely?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing faster is the speed at which I upvoted this skip. Nicely done, as always.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because all of those didn't work, I just left it as it's usual Euclid without change. And I changed the chamber size, I'm still not used to metric. Thanks for the analysis!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it's a paper-mache thing from a museum exhibit. I doubt I have the stomach to show an actual gorilla carcass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sick image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And with that, SCP-3836 goes fast into my heart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

I really enjoyed the article. The interview logs are cute and help to establish a reader-character bond. I think the beginning of the article has its put-offs, namely the containment procedures. A 6x6x8m chamber is enormous. Assuming it’s lxwxh, that would make the chamber 8 meters tall, which is about as high as a two story building. What does he need all that space for? He could probably just as easily survive in a 6x6x6m chamber. Also, when I read the revision early on, I jumped to thinking: “Why isn’t this Keter?” Because any object that the foundation CAN’T contain is generally classified Keter. However, after I read the rest of it it made more sense. I don’t know what I would do to the object class in your scenario. It’s not in containment, and it can’t be contained which would make it Keter. It’s not present around humanity and doesn’t pose a risk to normalcy (for all we know) so it probably wouldn’t be Keter. Neutralized wouldn’t work because it’s still active to the best of our knowledge, and Explained obviously wouldn’t work. I don’t know. I’m sure a veteran will be able to give more insight on the scenario than I can, but the object class confusion is no grounds for a downvote. The article is well written, well-thought out, and rightfully deserves a place on the mainlist in my opinion.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is what happens when I listen to Initial D while writing.

Pic is from Museum Boerhaave.

thanks to CadaverCommander for confirming that I'm not copying Joey Fucknuts and the rest of 3885. Though man, he'd get along well with the grease monkey here.

thanks UraniumEmpire, Weryllium, not_a_seagull

Update: We hit +69, wrap it up, everybody go home
